title: Libra
author: Don DeLillo
input_text: A postmodern historical novel centered on Lee Harvey Oswald, the Kennedy assassination, and the unstable boundary between documented history, conspiracy thinking, and fictional reconstruction. The interpretive emphasis is on how public events become unreadable when they are mediated through plots, archives, institutions, and competing narratives.
style_probe_instruction: If teaching style, ask students to add a short legally accessed passage from the novel and compare whether sentence texture, pacing, dialogue, and documentary framing change the agent scores.
source_note: Public reference summaries and the original prompt-dump cluster examples; instructor paraphrase, not copied prose.
rights_note: No copyrighted excerpt included. Students should use a legally accessed edition or instructor-provided fair-use excerpt.

title: House of Leaves
author: Mark Z. Danielewski
input_text: A layered metafictional novel built around a supposed documentary, The Navidson Record, a manuscript by Zampano, and editorial footnotes by Johnny Truant. Its central spatial premise is a house whose interior dimensions exceed its exterior, while its physical page design, typography, notes, appendices, and nested documents make reading itself part of the narrative difficulty.
style_probe_instruction: For style-sensitive analysis, students should inspect a legally accessed page spread and note typography, page layout, footnotes, visual spacing, and whether the formal design changes the Media/Structural Friction score.
source_note: Public reference summaries emphasize metafiction, nested narration, footnotes, appendices, and experimental layout.
rights_note: No copyrighted excerpt included. Use legally accessed page images or short classroom excerpts outside the public repo.

title: Gravity's Rainbow
author: Thomas Pynchon
input_text: An encyclopedic postmodern novel set around the end of World War II and the V-2 rocket program, following multiple intersecting plots, technical systems, war histories, corporate and military power, paranoia, science, sexuality, songs, jokes, and linguistic overload. The text is often treated as a difficult case of postmodern networked narration.
style_probe_instruction: If focusing on style, students should compare this summary with a legally accessed paragraph and ask whether the agent detects syntactic density, tonal switching, comic excess, technical language, and paranoid association differently.
source_note: Public reference summaries describe the V-2 setting, Schwarzgeraet plot element, complex structure, paranoia, and density.
rights_note: No copyrighted excerpt included. Students should use a legally accessed edition or instructor-provided fair-use excerpt.

title: Infinite Jest
author: David Foster Wallace
input_text: A long encyclopedic novel with an unconventional narrative structure, extensive endnotes, addiction and recovery settings, elite tennis training, entertainment culture, corporate saturation, and a near-future North American world organized around media obsession, loneliness, discipline, and compulsive attention.
style_probe_instruction: For style-sensitive work, students should add a legally accessed passage and ask whether the agent's affective score changes when exposed to Wallace's sentence-level recursion, comic detail, endnote logic, and tonal movement between irony and sincerity.
source_note: Public reference summaries emphasize unconventional structure, endnotes, addiction, entertainment, consumer culture, and scale.
rights_note: No copyrighted excerpt included. Use legally accessed editions or instructor-selected fair-use excerpts outside the public repo.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
input_text: A semi-autobiographical antiwar novel in which Billy Pilgrim moves unstably through time after his World War II experiences, including the bombing of Dresden. The book combines science fiction, war memory, black humor, repetition, simple syntax, metafictional framing, and traumatic detachment.
style_probe_instruction: For style-sensitive analysis, students should add a legally accessed short passage and test whether Vonnegut's simplicity, repetition, black humor, and abrupt temporal movement alter the Ontological Multiplicity and Paranoia/Affect scores.
source_note: Public reference summaries describe time travel, Dresden, antiwar framing, simple syntax, black humor, repetition, and metafiction.
rights_note: No copyrighted excerpt included. Use legally accessed editions or instructor-provided fair-use excerpts.
